L2.description: A vulnerability exists where artifact file paths received from a remote server response are used without proper validation in file system operations. An attacker who controls the server response can supply specially crafted paths to write or overwrite arbitrary files on the host filesystem, enabling remote code execution through overwriting configuration files such as shell initialization scripts.

L3.description: A vulnerability exists where artifact file paths received from a remote server response are used without proper validation in file system operations. An attacker who controls the server response can supply specially crafted paths to write or overwrite arbitrary files on the host filesystem, enabling remote code execution through overwriting configuration files such as shell initialization scripts.

ground_truth.reason: The list_artifacts method in the HTTP artifact repository processes file paths from a remote server response without validation, allowing path traversal to write arbitrary files and achieve command execution.
